question_text: An electric bell uses an electromagnet that switches on and off. When the electromagnet switches off, a spring pushes the metal arm back. Why is it important that the electromagnet can be switched off quickly?

options.label: A
options.text: So the bell can ring continuously and produce a loud ringing sound

options.label: B
options.text: So the electromagnet doesn't get too hot and break

options.label: C
options.text: So the battery lasts longer and doesn't run out of charge

options.label: D
options.text: So the electromagnet becomes a permanent magnet instead of temporary

explanation: When the electromagnet switches off, the spring pulls the arm back, which breaks the circuit and switches the electromagnet off again. This on-off cycle repeats rapidly, making the bell hammer strike the bell repeatedly and create a ringing sound. Without quick switching, the arm would just stay pulled once and the bell would only ring once.
